Corecore is a unique trend that appeared on TikTok and quickly caught people’s attention. Unlike many other TikTok trends that focus on dancing, comedy, or challenges, corecore is more about commentary on internet culture itself. It usually combines random clips, memes, advertisements, and viral moments into a single video to show how chaotic and overwhelming the internet can feel. Many people see it as both a joke and a critique of modern social media.

O Dreamcore é como estar preso dentro de um sonho que você não lembra direito. É familiar, mas estranho. Bonito, mas meio triste. São lugares vazios, sons distantes, luzes suaves — tudo parece real, mas algo não encaixa. É a sensação de nostalgia por algo que talvez nunca existiu. Corecore (корекор) — это интернет-эстетика и художественное движение, популярное в соцсетях (особенно в TikTok), которое сочетает случайные видеоклипы, мемы, отрывки из фильмов и музыки для создания сильного эмоционального отклика, часто ностальгического, сюрреалистического или меланхоличного, подчеркивая абсурдность и экзистенциальные проблемы современного мира, как одиночество на фоне изобилия контента.

Bliss, originally titled Bucolic Green Hills, is the default computer wallpaper of Microsoft’s Windows XP operating system. It is an unedited photograph of a green hill and blue sky with white clouds in the Los Carneros American Viticultural Area of California’s Wine Country. Charles O’Rear took the photo in January 1996 and Microsoft bought the rights in 2000. It is estimated that billions of people have seen the picture, possibly making it the most viewed photograph in history.

a "loser" is a derogatory term for someone seen as habitually unsuccessful, incompetent, socially awkward, or generally lacking in desirable qualities like drive, status, or friends, often implying they are a misfit or someone to be looked down upon.
